column4: Telfah et al. 2023 Fabrication and characterization section; supplier-identified N42 sintered NdFeB, nickel-coated cubes used in the multilayer Halbach magnet
column5: The paper reports 1350 mT remanence, 923 kA/m coercivity, and 318.3 kJ/m3 maximum energy product but does not state whether these are measured values or supplier nominal values. Magnetic test method, property test temperature, intrinsic coercivity, recoil permeability, demagnetization curves, temperature coefficients, cube-to-value allocation, lot, chemistry, sinter schedule, coating stack/thickness, and dispersion are absent. The same sentence also prints 42 MGOe for maximum energy product, which is not SI-equivalent to 318.3 kJ/m3; the conflicting representations remain separate claims

column4: Exact unit normalization of the separately printed 42 MGOe maximum-energy-product representation using 1 G=1e-4 T and 1 Oe=1000/(4*pi) A/m
column5: 42 MGOe converts to 334.2253804929802 kJ/m3, not the 318.3 kJ/m3 printed in the same source sentence. This derived SI transcription exists only to preserve and expose the source conflict; it is not a refit, correction, preference, uncertainty interval, or independent observation. Source magnetic test temperature and method remain absent
